task_completion.description: Import the required photos and audio into a video editor
task_completion.visual_evidence: A video editing application (e.g., Kdenlive, OpenShot, Pitivi) is open, and the project bin or timeline shows the 8 photos (room_01.jpg to room_08.jpg) and the audio file (property_narration.wav).

task_completion.description: Assemble the photos and audio on the timeline
task_completion.visual_evidence: The video editor's timeline shows the 8 image clips arranged sequentially on a video track, with the audio file placed on an audio track underneath them.
The timeline displays visual indicators of transitions (crossfades) between the image clips, as well as fade-in/fade-out effects at the beginning and end of the sequence.

task_completion.description: Export the video in the specified formats and resolutions
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window or terminal shows the /home/ga/Videos/listing_output/ directory containing the exported video files (master, mobile, square, email, and thumbnail).
The manifest.json file is visible in the /home/ga/Videos/listing_output/ directory, or is shown open in a text editor/terminal displaying the properties of the exported files.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical video editing application to assemble the video
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with a GUI video editor (like Kdenlive, OpenShot, or Pitivi) rather than the agent exclusively using terminal commands (like ffmpeg) to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The agent actually rendered the videos from the editor
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the video editor's render/export dialog, settings window, or a rendering progress bar, indicating the outputs were genuinely generated from the assembled timeline.

integrity.description: The agent actively created the manifest.json file
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (like gedit, nano, or VS Code) or terminal commands where the agent is actively writing or generating the JSON content, rather than copying a pre-existing file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires exactly 6 expected outputs in /home/ga/Videos/listing_output/. The target resolutions for the 5 video/image deliverables are: 1920x1080 (master), 1280x720 (mobile), 1080x1080 (square), 640x360 (email), and 400x300 (thumbnail). The 6th output is the manifest.json file.
